name: overlap_zero_size_reflexive
reason: Two zero-size regions at the same base are considered overlapping via the `region1.base == region2.base` branch — empty intervals should not overlap

name: overlap_not_symmetric
code:
proof fn phi_2_overlap_not_symmetric(a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        a < b,
        b < a + 1,
    ensures
        overlap(MemRegion { base: a, size: 1 }, MemRegion { base: b, size: 0 }),
        !overlap(MemRegion { base: b, size: 0 }, MemRegion { base: a, size: 1 }),
{
}
reason: `overlap` is not symmetric — a zero-size region inside a non-zero region is detected in one direction but not the other

            Map::empty().insert(base, pte),

}

name: monotonic_does_not_require_key_preservation
code:
proof fn phi_4_monotonic_does_not_require_key_preservation(base: nat, pte1: PTE, pte2: PTE)
    requires
        pte1.frame.size > 0,
        pte2.frame.size > 0,
        overlap(MemRegion { base: base, size: pte1.frame.size }, MemRegion { base: base, size: pte2.frame.size }),
    ensures
        candidate_mapping_overlaps_existing_vmem(
            Map::empty().insert(base, pte1).insert(base, pte2),
            base,
            pte2,
        ),
{
}
reason: Submap monotonicity holds even when mappings2 overwrites a key from mappings1 — the lemma's `submap_of` precondition doesn't account for value changes at existing keys

name: overlap_asymmetric_containment
code:
proof fn phi_5_overlap_asymmetric_containment(a: nat, sz: nat)
    requires
        sz > 0,
    ensures
        overlap(MemRegion { base: a, size: sz }, MemRegion { base: a + sz, size: 0 }),
        !overlap(MemRegion { base: a + sz, size: 0 }, MemRegion { base: a, size: sz }),
{
}
reason: A zero-size region at the exclusive upper bound of another region is detected as overlapping in one direction but not the reverse — `overlap` is asymmetric at boundary points
